factors.name: Economic Inequality
factors.description: Measures the concentration of wealth and income disparities. Elevated inequality correlates with historical preconditions for mass unrest.

factors.name: Political Polarization
factors.description: Tracks the degree of ideological divergence between major political factions. High polarization reduces the possibility of negotiated resolution to grievances.

factors.name: Protest Activity
factors.description: Tracks frequency, size, and geographic spread of organized public demonstrations. A leading indicator of mass mobilization capacity.

factors.name: Institutional Trust
factors.description: Measures public confidence in government, judiciary, media, and law enforcement. Declining trust removes the legitimating mechanisms that prevent political violence.

factors.name: Unemployment & Economic Stress
factors.description: Combines unemployment rate, underemployment, and household economic insecurity. Economic precarity creates conditions for political radicalization.
